verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p23/TMP21) is a Type I single-pass TM protein of the p24 family whose dominant localization is the ER–ERGIC–Golgi axis, where it functions as a cargo receptor in COPI/COPII vesicular trafficking. However, multiple sources explicitly document a plasma membrane pool alongside Golgi cisternae. As a Type I TM protein, its large luminal GOLD domain faces the extracellular space when at the PM, making the protein body accessible from the outer leaflet. The PM pool is real but minority; the dominant compartment is endomembrane. This dual localization (dominant Golgi/ER + minority PM) qualifies as contextual/dual_localization.
